title: Southwest Washington 211 Services Now Managed by WA 211 - 211info
text: As of January 1, 2026, 211info serves all 36 counties in Oregon, and Washington 211 (WA 211) serves communities in Southwest Washington. This change reflects WA 211’s transition from multiple regional 211 offices to a centralized, statewide hub. Oregon already operates under a similar model, with a single point of coordination statewide. 211info previously managed 211 services in Clark and Skamania counties beginning in 2004, and in Cowlitz and Wahkiakum counties beginning in 2015. With Washington’s new hub model, WA 211 now serves these counties directly. What this means for people calling 211 For individuals and families, nothing changes. Calling 211 still connects you to resources in your community. Calls are automatically routed to the appropriate local 211, based on where you are calling from. Accessing Southwest Washington resources by text or email is now managed by WA 211: Text: the keyword “211WAOD” to 898211 Email: WA 211 uses a webform over direct email What this means for service providers and partners Service providers and partners should update Washington-based resources directly with WA 211 . Providers should continue updating Oregon-based resources with 211info. Programs or services available to residents in both Oregon and Washington should connect with both 211info and WA 211 to ensure listings are accurate. 211info and WA 211 continue to work closely as partners, and are aligned in our shared goal of connecting people to the help they need. We congratulate WA 211 on their transition to a statewide hub model and look forward to continued collaboration. For more information about WA 211’s new hub model, check out their website .
